violations.rule: Back brevity test — aim for under 10 words, no 'and' joining two facts
violations.detail: Back is 12 words: "Every read receives the most recent write or an error". Could be trimmed.

violations.rule: Back brevity test — aim for under 10 words
violations.detail: Back is 11 words: "The system continues operating despite network message loss between nodes". Could be trimmed.

violations.rule: Back brevity test — aim for under 10 words
violations.detail: Back is 9 words: "Network partitions are inevitable in real distributed environments". Borderline but acceptable.

violations.rule: Back brevity test — aim for under 10 words
violations.detail: Back is 14 words: "All replicas will converge to the same value given enough time without new writes". Should be trimmed significantly.

violations.rule: Back brevity test — parenthetical adding a NEW fact
violations.detail: Back is "2002 (by Gilbert and Lynch)" — the parenthetical adds who proved it, which is a separate fact from when. Should be split into two cards or the parenthetical removed.

violations.rule: Atomic — back has two facts joined
violations.detail: Back is "It sacrifices availability (may return errors)" — the parenthetical adds an elaboration/consequence beyond the core answer. Should be just "Availability" or the elaboration should be a separate card.

violations.rule: Atomic — back has two facts joined
violations.detail: Back is "It sacrifices consistency (may return stale data)" — same issue as card 10. The parenthetical adds a new fact about what sacrificing consistency means in practice.
